events.title: Fixed audio mixer gain and planned memory folder features
events.summary: The user worked on multiple My-Meeting refinements with Claude. The audio mixer gain was fixed for dual-source recording in ProfessionalAudioMixer.swift: when both mic and system audio are active, direct summing causes clipping and raises the noise floor, so a 0.7x gain is now applied via vDSP; for single-source mode (mic-only or system-off), the gain stays at 1.0x to avoid unnecessary volume redu
events.tags: my-meeting, swift, audio-mixer, gain, dual-source, memory-folder, configstore, claude-code, terminal

events.tags: my-portrait, swift, audio-processing, transcription-queue, zombie-chunk, bug-fixing, claude-code, terminal

events.title: Learned about writing style facet extraction pipeline
events.summary: The user asked Claude about how the writing style extraction pipeline works in My-Portrait. Claude explained the two-layer process: raw typing events are first cleaned into records, then LLM analysis produces writing style facets grouped into five categories (chat brevity, delete-burst revision, bilingual register split, etc.). Each facet is stored as an individual Markdown file under portrait/wri
events.tags: my-portrait, writing-style, memory-pipeline, portrait-facets, llm-analysis, claude-code, terminal

events.title: Learned about Xcode multi-project window capabilities
events.summary: The user asked Claude whether Xcode can only open one project at a time, concerned about needing to close a project to test another on the same device. Claude clarified that Xcode supports opening multiple projects in separate independent windows via File > Open, each with its own editor, scheme selector, and run/stop controls. Claude also explained that a single iOS device can run and debug multi
events.tags: xcode, claude, development-tools, ios, debugging
